summary: Excellent FM broadcast of a New Year's Eve show – solid sound despite slight static and standout performances from Sugar Magnolia through Johnny B. Goode.
review: The recording captures the legendary late‑1978 NYE concert with a mostly clean FM broadcast mix (minor static and occasional low‑level guest bleed) that remains very listenable. Musically the band is in top form – opening hot with Sugar Magnolia, delivering a mellow Scarlet > Fire jam, an epic Friend of the Devil, a classic third‑set mini‑suite (Dark Star → The Other One → Dark Star Jam → Wharf Rat → Saint Stephen → Good Lovin'), and closing with a blistering Johnny B. Goode that showcases Jerry’s fiercest guitar work.

ai_rating.rationale: Five‑star and four‑star reviews consistently praise the performance and only note minor static in the FM broadcast; the show is highlighted as one of the best NYE sets, justifying a high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Partial FM broadcast (Bertha DAW) with occasional slight static and low‑level guest mixes; overall clear and balanced, no major dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set marathon starting at midnight; the first set builds with hot openers, the second set stretches to a 1:45 jam marathon, and the third set delivers a classic mini‑suite that caps the night perfectly.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone is praised throughout – especially on Johnny B. Goode (called his hardest rock) and the Dark Star/Other One sequences.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s slide work and vocal quirks are highlighted (“Bobby’s slide practice”, “Bobby screams into mic”), generally viewed positively.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard moments noted – an Itsy‑Bitsy lick into Casey Jones and solid support throughout the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming is steady; the Drums segment includes Kesey’s Thunder Machine, though its mic level is unclear.
